qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Empty-permission API key can execute custom-function preview and disclose workspace variables
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Mismatch: this finding is a missing permission check on `POST /api/v1/node-custom-function` that lets an authenticated low-privilege API key execute preview JavaScript and read workspace variables, while the advisory is unsanitized `customReadCSV` interpolation in `packages/components/nodes/agents/CSVAgent/CSVAgent.ts` that reaches `pyodide.runPythonAsync(code)` through the chatflow prediction path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding in this session identifies the advisory's CSVAgent root cause. The advisory is an authenticated code-injection flaw where attacker-controlled `customReadCSV` input is interpolated into Python and executed by `pyodide.runPythonAsync(code)` during the `/api/v1/chatflows` plus `/api/v1/prediction/:id` flow. The only finalized qualifying finding instead covers a separate RBAC bypass on the custom-function preview endpoint.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes `customReadCSVFunc` in `CSVAgent.ts` being interpolated into a `code` string and executed by `pyodide.runPythonAsync(code)`, with exploitation through chatflow creation and `/api/v1/prediction/[CHATFLOWID]`.

The only real qualifying finding in `.judge/input_manifest.json` and `custom_function_preview_authz_bypass/vulnerability_report.json` targets `POST /api/v1/node-custom-function`, `executeCustomNodeFunction`, and `CustomFunction.ts`, and its proven impact is workspace-secret disclosure to an authenticated empty-permission API key.

The finding report never identifies `CSVAgent.ts`, `customReadCSV`, `pyodide.runPythonAsync`, or the chatflow prediction execution chain that defines the advisory-described vulnerable code and root cause.
